titleStmt.title: SII 22.264: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 264. (A.R. No. 264 of 1906) NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT, CHEYYAR TALUK, NARASAMAṄGALAM. ON A THIRD STONE SETUP ON THE BUND OF THE TANK.

summary.p: This is a Sanskrit inscription engraved in Nāgari characters and is dated in Śaka 1560, Bahudhānya corresponding to A.D. 1638-39. It gives high praise to Veṅkaṭappa Nāyaka the donor of the Chennasāgara tank. He is said to have been a member of the Dāmarla family, of which the earliest mentioned was one Abba-Mahīpati. His great grand-father was Vēṅgala-Bhūpa and grand-father Veṅkaṭa-Bhūpa. His father was Chenna-Mahēndra “the vanquisher of his foes” and his mother was called Kṛishṇamāmbā. This Veṅkaṭa himself was a great conqueror, and was noted for his beauty, learning, courage and liberality. He claims to have defeated in battle the Cheñji chief of the time and to have spread his fame in all directions. He constructed at Māvaḍūr-agrahāra a big tank called Chennasāgara after his father, and channels to feed the same from the river and from the tank to irrigate the lands situated in 32 villages round about the place. He had a younger brother by name Akka-nṛipa whom he calls his right arm and who was also known as Nava-Bhōja because of his gift of the Muses.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śrīḥ śrīrastu . Asti prakāmaṁ jagatīha jātapraśasti dāmarlakulaṁ viśālaṁ .
yasmin mahatyabbamahīpamukhyāḥ kṛtāvatārāḥ kṣitipālamukhyāḥ . 1 tatra
hi prathitaveṁgalabhūpā dvṛtta devavibhavairupapannaḥ . najani veṁkaṭa
bhūpo tyamaniśaṁ sukṛtaṁ ca . 2 tasmādudītastanayaḥ prajānāmadhīdha
raścennamahīmaheṁdraḥ . jitāriṇā nā tāṁgagarvanirvāpaṇam yena kṛtaṁ raṇā
gre . 3 vidveṣivargamacirādakhilaṁ vijitya nāmamahitāṁ nijarājadhānīṁ .
Āvāsya (?) yena mahatālamabhāji sītāṁ purā raghuvareṇa nirudhya (?) laṁkāṁ [. 4*]
bhuvanamahitacaryā bhūpatestasya bhāryā sakalaguṇanidhiḥ kṛṣṇamāṁbāna
vadyā . vimalaguṇaviśālaṁ veṁkaṭakṣoṇipālaṁ garjaśālaṁ
. 5 yatsauṁdaryamanaṁgavismayakaraṁ yacchaurya pratyarthi
kṣitipāla vulamahā haṁ kāra huṁkārakaṁ . yatpāṁḍitya deśika
sāragarvāpahaṁ yaddātṛtvamatīva karmasurabhi vahaṁ vartate . 6 saṁkhe (khye) niḥ
saṁkhya turagakarighaṭāsaṁvṛtaṁ maṁḍaleṁdraṁ ceṁji kṣmāpāla mudya
dviradamavasirā (?) vyāghnakaṁṭhīraveṁdraḥ . yaśchiṁdan kīrtipūrānvyakiradadhidi
śaṁ taddhanurmauktikābhā vijayi nijabhujāvikramā
krāṁtalokaḥ . 7 pratyarthipṛthvīpati siṁhāsana sthāpana
śauryaḥ . yo devarājāṁghrisarojayugma makaraṁda bhṛṁgī bhavadaṁtaraṁgaḥ . 8
pāruṣyavāci parihāsakathāṁtarepi rāvi
rāsīt . mahāmahatyapi sulame parasya svapnepi yasya viratonya
vadhūprasaṁgaḥ . 9 yamanyanārīsahajaṁ vadaṁti manīṣiṇa [statvamapūrvamatya ?]
. 10 bhuvana
vinutakāṁcī puṇyakoṭī vimāna praṇati karaṇa dhanyairbhāsamāne su
vipraiḥ . mahivaratanuhāre māvaḍūraprahāre vipulatarataṭākaṁ veṁka
ṭeṁdro vyatānīt . 11 kacittadativāḍavaḥ kacana kuṁbhajanmā kaci
tkapiyuto raghu pūra vaniḥ . Upaplava
payodhiriha . 13
mānasaṁ tajjalāsvādamāsī pahasanniva potaḥ .
todakasamṛddhimanaṁta ceṁnasamudraḥ . 14
kāvāhinīḥ svavaśayan yathā vibhuḥ . sakala
vito jayati ceṁnasāgaraḥ . 15 śrī śālivāhanaśaka prabhuvatsarāṇāṁ ṣa
ṣṭayuttare sagaṇiterdhayute sahasre . vaiśākhamāsyakalayadbahudhānya va
ṣeṁ śrīcennasāgara Iti prathitaṁ taṭākaṁ . 16 dvātniṁśadgrāmasīmāsamadhi
kakalamājjīvakānekakulyāvalyā pūrṇābupūrasthirataṭanikaṭā
māmaḍūragrahārā . śrīma kaṁṭisiṁha birudavatā veṁkaṭeṁdreṇa kā
jayatu rācaṁdratāraṁ . 17 śrīrastu .
Aśeṣavibudhaślādhyairamṛtāpairaharniśaṁ . rājamaṁḍalavatpūrṇṇā rāja
te ceṁnnasāgaraḥ . 18 dakṣiṇabhuja Iva śrīmat veṁkaṭadharaṇīpateḥ pri
yo bhrātā . Akkanṛpaḥ satkavitā navabhojo jayatu śauryakīrtinidhiḥ . 19
veṁkaṭavibhuhṛdayavidā vaidikalaukika viśeṣavi jñe na . likhitā
tamidaṁ viṣayī perū bhaṭṭena . 20 yośca kulyā
. 21
Asya dharmasya yo vighna kuryādbhuvi narādhamaḥ . sagaṁtā svasya jananīṁ
svasāramapi putrikāṁ . 22 ceṁnasāgara dveṁkaṭabhūpatiḥ . sā
nujaḥ sasutaḥ śrīmān ciraṁ jīvatu dhārmikaḥ . 23 divākarādhvarī
myaṁ nakha ṭārthakaiḥ . ślokairalikhatāmetacchāsanaṁ ceṁnasāgaraṁ . 24 śubha
mastu .
Note:—I am thankful to the Government Epigraphist for this revised reading of my transcript.
Digital edition of SII 22.264 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
